title: Pringle Cottage
diff_added:
The eastern facade of the cottage, which faces Dragon Street, is dominated by the [[ogee]] or double curved corrugated iron verandah, supported on chamfered timber posts. The returns of the verandah, above the line of the posts are infilled with timber lattice panels. A centrally located half glazed entrance door is flanked by two pane vertical [[sash window]] openings. These openings are all surmounted by [[Transom (architectural)|transom lights]], narrow over the windows and more generous above the door.<ref name=qhr/>
The interior rooms of the ground floor feature early chimney pieces, mantle pieces and [[cast iron]] fire [[Grille (architecture)|grille]]s. The rooms are generally quite simple, with timber skirting boards, stained [[architrave]]s and stained timber boarded ceilings.<ref name=qhr/>

diff_added: Over the three nights of the Blitz a total of nearly 14 hours of enemy activity were recorded. 1,273 high explosive bombs and 56,000 [[Incendiary device|Incendiary bombs]] were estimated to have been dropped over an area measuring approximately 41 acres.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.swanseamuseum.co.uk/swansea-a-brief-history/world-war-two/the-blitz|title=The Blitz|website=Swanseamuseum.co.uk|date=21 February 1941|access-date=9 August 2017}}</ref> The town centre suffered direct hits that started major conflagrations destroying many commercial premises including the Ben Evans department store and the Victorian market.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/looking-back-swansea-blitz-1941-1858161|title=Looking back at the Swansea blitz of 1941|publisher=Wales Online|date=19 February 1941|access-date=9 August 2017}}</ref> The death toll after the three days of bombing stood at 230: 122 men, 68 women and 37 children. There were 254 serious injuries and 137 slight injuries reported. Altogether 282 houses were destroyed and some 7000 left homeless. 574 business premises were destroyed and 11,054 damaged.<ref>Alban, J.R. (1994) ''The Three Nights’'' Blitz. Swansea: Swansea City Council. p. 17.</ref> To raise morale following the Blitz, there were visits to Swansea by [[George VI of the United Kingdom|the King]] and [[Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother|Queen]] in March 1941, and the prime minister, [[Winston Churchill]], the following month.<ref>{{cite book |title =Swansea and the Second World War| last =Lewis| first=Bernard| year =2024|page= 175-76| location =Talybont| publisher =Y Lolfa|isbn=9781800994409}}</ref>

title: Elisabet Sahtouris
comment: still needs some sources but i tried to at least clean up the grammar a bit and reflect that she'd died
diff_added:
'''Elisabet Sahtouris''' (January 23, 1936 - December 21, 2024)<ref>{{cite web | title=Remembering Elisabet Sahtouris | website=Science and Nonduality (SAND) | date=2025-01-08 | url=https://scienceandnonduality.com/article/remembering-elisabet-sahtouris/ | access-date=2026-02-13}}</ref> was an evolutionary biologist, futurist, speaker, author and sustainability consultant. She was a US and Greek citizen who lived in the US, Canada, Greece, Peru and Spain while lecturing, doing workshops and media appearances on all continents. She earned a PhD from [[Dalhousie University]] in Canada. During her lifetime, she consulted with corporations and government organizations in [[Australia]], [[Brazil]], Europe, Asia, Africa and the [[United States]].
Sahtouris co-convened two symposia on the foundations of science in Hokkaido and Kuala Lumpur. She was Professor in Residence at [[Chaminade University of Honolulu|Chaminade University in Honolulu]], Hawaii, teaching in the School of Business & Communication MBA Program and helping redesign it for entrepreneurship in local living economies. She was a member of the Evolutionary Leaders and a founding member of Rising Women; Rising World.
She appeared in films including ''Occupy Love, I Am, Femme, Love Thy Nature ''and'' Money & Life.'' Her books include ''EarthDance: Living Systems in Evolution, A Walk Through Time: from Stardust to Us,''  ''Biology Revisioned'' (with Willis Harman), and ''Gaia’s Dance: The Story of Earth & Us''.

She was a US and Greek citizen who has lived in the US, Canada, Greece, Peru and Spain while lecturing, doing workshops and media appearances on all continents. She has a PhD from [[Dalhousie University]] in Canada. She consulted with corporations and government organizations in [[Australia]], [[Brazil]], Europe, Asia, Africa and the [[United States]].
Sahtouris co-convened two symposia on the foundations of science in Hokkaido and Kuala Lumpur. She is currently Professor in Residence at [[Chaminade University of Honolulu|Chaminade University in Honolulu]], Hawaii, teaching in the School of Business & Communication MBA Program and helping redesign it for entrepreneurship in local living economies. She is a member of the Evolutionary Leaders and a founding member of Rising Women; Rising World. She promotes a vision she believes will result in the sustainable health and well-being of humanity within the larger living systems of Earth and the cosmos.
She has appeared in films including ''Occupy Love, I Am, Femme, Love Thy Nature and Money & Life.'' Her books include ''EarthDance: Living Systems in Evolution, A Walk Through Time: from Stardust to Us,''  ''Biology Revisioned'' (with Willis Harman). and new ebook ''Gaia’s Dance: The Story of Earth & Us''.

title: Asclepias ruthiae
diff_added:
'''''Asclepias ruthiae''''' is a species of flowering plant in the family [[Apocynaceae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Utah]] in the western [[United States]], where it grows in [[desert]] and [[shrubland]] habitats.<ref name="ns" /><ref name="powo" />
''Asclepias ruthiae'' is a [[perennial]] [[herb]] with multiple [[plant stem|stems]] arising from a [[caudex]]. The stems are suberect and reach approximately 10–15 cm in height. [[Leaves]] are broadly ovate, 2.2–4.5 cm long, with a pubescent surface and short [[leaf petiole|petioles]].<ref name="Maguire1941"/>
The [[inflorescences]] are small, [[umbel]]-like clusters bearing 2–7 flowers. The flowers have a pale grayish-violet [[corolla (botany)|corolla]] with reflexed lobes and a distinctive [[corona (botany)|corona]] structure. [[Fruits]] are erect, curved [[follicle (fruit)|follicles]] measuring up to 4–5 cm in length.<ref name="Maguire1941" />
The species is restricted to central [[Utah]], particularly the [[San Rafael Swell]] region.<ref name="ns" /><ref name="powo" /> It occurs in [[desert]] [[shrublands]], including [[saltbush]] communities and [[pinyon pine|pinyon-juniper]] [[woodlands]], and is sometimes found along drainages.<ref name="ns" />
''Asclepias ruthiae'' is ranked as G3 (vulnerable) by [[NatureServe]].<ref name="ns" /> It is known from more than 24 occurrences, though overall abundance and trends are poorly understood.<ref name="ns" />
Potential threats include livestock [[grazing]] and roadside maintenance activities, although their impacts have not been fully quantified.<ref name="ns" /> The species occurs in some protected areas, including [[Capitol Reef National Park]] and [[Bureau of Land Management]] lands in the [[San Rafael Swell]].<ref name="ns" />

title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream
comment: may not be formatted right, but included info from previews regarding pronouns and dating preferences
diff_added: Like its predecessors, ''Living the Dream'' is a [[social simulation game]] about the lives of interactable [[Mii]] characters living on a remote tropical island.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Fanelli|first1=Jason|title=''Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream'' Coming To Nintendo Switch In 2026|url=https://www.gamespot.com/articles/tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-coming-to-nintendo-switch-in-2026/1100-6530426/|website=[[GameSpot]]|access-date=27 March 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Stewart|first1=Marcus|title=''Tomodachi Life'' Returns After 10 Years With New Entry, ''Living The Dream''|url=https://www.gameinformer.com/nintendo-direct/2025/03/27/tomodachi-life-returns-after-10-years-with-new-entry-living-the-dream|website=[[Game Informer]]|access-date=28 March 2025|date=27 March 2025|archive-date=28 March 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250328040959/https://gameinformer.com/nintendo-direct/2025/03/27/tomodachi-life-returns-after-10-years-with-new-entry-living-the-dream|url-status=live}}</ref> As Miis live their daily lives on the island, the player can observe and interact with the Miis, including buying and gifting them clothing, food, and decorations. ''Living the Dream'' also introduces the ability to customize the layout and shape of the island. Unlike in previous entries, the game allows players to directly control interactions between Miis rather than relying on random chance for Miis to develop their relationships.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Henley|first=Stacey|date=2025-09-12|title=''Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream''{{'}}s New Mechanic Can Bring Your Fanfiction To Life|url=https://www.thegamer.com/tomodachi-life-living-the-dreams-mechanic-friendship/|access-date=2025-10-16|website=TheGamer|language=en}}</ref> Additionally, new options for the in-game [[Mii#Mii creation|Mii Maker]] allow more advanced customization, including new options for hair, facial features, and ears.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Carmona|first=Carla|date=2025-04-02|title=''Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream''{{'}}s Miis Look Cursed Due to This Change|url=https://gamerant.com/tomodachi-life-living-the-dreams-miis-cursed-why-ears/|access-date=2025-10-16|website=Game Rant|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Nintendo Direct Announced For This Week, Focused on Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/nintendo-direct-announced-for-this-week-focused-on-tomodachi-life-living-the-dream|website=[[IGN]]|date=2026-01-26|access-date=2026-01-30|language=en|first=Tom|last=Phillips}}</ref> ''Living the Dream'' is also the first entry in the series to allow [[non-binary]] Miis, [[Aromanticism|aromantic]] Miis, customizable pronouns and clothing preferences{{Citation needed|date=March 2026}}, [[same-sex relationships]], with the player being able to customize Miis' dating preferences.<ref name=":3" /><ref name=":4">{{Cite news|title=Nintendo expands Mii options with non-binary choice in Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news

title: Artabanus II of Parthia
diff_added: Before his ascension to the Parthian throne, Artabanus had ruled as king of [[Media Atropatene]], which later served as his base of attacks against the [[Roman Empire|Roman]]-supported Parthian king Vonones I. Artabanus eventually defeated Vonones I, who fled to Armenia and became its king. Artabanus' efforts to replace Vonones I with his son were blocked by the Romans, who eventually reached an agreement with the Parthians to appoint [[Artaxias III]] the new king of Armenia and renounce their support of Vonones. It was a symbol for aliens to reach home. I.

title: Child abuse in China
comment: rv lead to pre-AI version (the text is not only likely AI but, per the edit summary, unreviewed)
diff_added: '''Child abuse in China''' is not clearly defined by the Chinese legal code.
diff_removed: '''Child abuse in the People's Republic of China''' is the [[Child abuse|maltreatment]], [[Child neglect|neglect]], or exploitation of children, encompassing [[physical abuse]], [[emotional abuse]], [[sexual abuse]], and [[child labor]]. Despite increasing public awareness of and government initiatives to tackle child abuse, reports indicate high rates of abuse and neglect among children, with societal attitudes and traditional [[Confucianism|Confucian values]] complicating efforts to establish a robust [[child protection]] framework. Furthermore, the [[law of the People's Republic of China]] does not systematically define or codify child abuse, which critically challenges the extent to which vulnerable children can be protected. Despite recent efforts to combat child abuse, including the creation of [[sex offender registry|sex offender registries]] and [[mandatory reporting]] systems, significant gaps in protection and support remain.

In calculations, the all-silicon cyclic hexasilabenzene Si<sub>6</sub>H<sub>6</sub> is variously predicted to have 6-fold symmetry<ref>{{Cite journal| doi = 10.1021/ja00839a005| title = Ground States of Molecules. XXIX. MINDO/3 Calculations of Compounds Containing Third Row Elements| year = 1975| last1 = Dewar | first1 = M. J. S.| last2 = Lo | first2 = D. H.| last3 = Ramsden | first3 = C. A.| journal = Journal of the American Chemical Society| volume = 97| issue = 6| pages = 1311–1318}}</ref> a [[chair conformation]].<ref>{{Cite journal| doi = 10.1063/1.452726| title = Hexasilabenzene (Si<sub>6</sub>H<sub>6</sub>). Is the Benzene-Like ''D''<sub>6h</sub> Structure Stable?| year = 1987| last1 = Nagase  | first1 = S.| last2 = Teramae | first2 = H.| last3 = Kudo | first3 = T.| journal = The Journal of Chemical Physics| volume = 86| issue = 8| pages = 4513–4517  | bibcode = 1987JChPh..86.4513N}}</ref> It was shown that the deviation from planarity in hexasilabenzene is caused by the [[pseudo Jahn–Teller effect]].<ref>{{cite journal|last=Ivanov|first=A.|author2=Boldyrev. A |title=Si<sub>6−n</sub>C<sub>n</sub>H<sub>6</sub> (n = 0-6) Series: When Do Silabenzenes Become Planar and Global Minima?|journal=J. Phys. Chem. A|volume=116|issue=38|pages=9591–9598|year=2012|doi=10.1021/jp307722q|pmid=22946702|bibcode=2012JPCA..116.9591I}}</ref>
The searching of a planar Si<sub>6</sub> analogue to benzene has been extended to anionic cycles and structures bearing lithium atoms replacing hydrogens.<ref>{{cite journal|last1=Takahasi|first1=M|last2=Kawazoe|first2=Y|title=Theoretical Study on Planar Anionic Polysilicon Chains and Cyclic Si<sub>6</sub>H Anions with ''D''<sub>6h</sub> Symmetry|journal=Organometallics|date=2005|volume=24|issue=10|pages=2433–2440|doi=10.1021/om050025c}}</ref> Using [[density functional theory]], it has been shown that from a series of planar and tridimensional structures with molecular formula Si<sub>6</sub>Li<sub>2-8</sub>, the global minimum is a Si<sub>6</sub>Li<sub>6</sub> planar ring. This particular ring has ''D''<sub>2h</sub> symmetry with 4 lithium cations placed between two adjacent silicon atoms –forming [[three-center two-electron bond]]s –and two more Li cations located above and below the center of the ring’s plane. title: Time Masters (game show)
diff_added: '''''Time Masters''''' is an Australian children's [[game show]] hosted by [[Tony Johnston]] from 1996 to 1998 on the [[Seven Network]].<ref name="best kids shows">{{Cite web |date=2018-06-30 |title=The Best Aussie Kids Game Shows from the '90s |url=http://www.cactuspop.com/aussie-kids-game-shows/ |access-date=2020-02-17 |website=Cactus Pop}}</ref> Running for three seasons, Tony would meet the two school teams consisting of two players each. The teams then participated in three rounds of games, competing for points. After five days of play, the winning school would take home the major prize, whilst the losing school would take home a runners up prize. In each of the five daily episodes, players won player's prizes, and for Scramble, the fastest player also won a watch. In 1998, the show ended and was replaced with ''[[Wipeout (1999 game show)|Wipeout]]'' a year later, also hosted by Johnston.

The teams then participated in three rounds of games, competing for points. After five days of play, the winning school would take home the major prize, whilst the losing school would take home a runners up prize. In each of the five daily episodes, players won player's prizes, and for Scramble, the fastest player also won a watch. In 1998, the show ended and was replaced with ''[[Wipeout (1999 game show)|Wipeout]]'' a year later, also hosted by Johnston.

title: Artabanus II of Parthia
comment: Reverting possible vandalism by [[Special:Contribs/Dr. Harvey James|Dr. Harvey James]] to version by GreenC bot. [[WP:CBFP|Report False Positive?]] Thanks, [[WP:CBNG|ClueBot NG]]. (4496347) (Bot)
diff_added: Before his ascension to the Parthian throne, Artabanus had ruled as king of [[Media Atropatene]], which later served as his base of attacks against the [[Roman Empire|Roman]]-supported Parthian king Vonones I. Artabanus eventually defeated Vonones I, who fled to Armenia and became its king. Artabanus' efforts to replace Vonones I with his son were blocked by the Romans, who eventually reached an agreement with the Parthians to appoint [[Artaxias III]] the new king of Armenia and renounce their support of Vonones I.

title: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
diff_added: At Vice President [[James Killian]]'s urging, MIT faculty formed a Committee on Educational Survey chaired by chemical engineer [[Warren K. Lewis]].{{Sfn|Killian|1985|pp=79–80}}{{refn|group=lower-alpha|Killian, appointed president a year later in 1948, promoted the adoption of the committee's ideas.}} After two years of study, the Lewis Committee produced a landmark report in 1949.{{Sfn|Geiger|1993|p=65}} It reaffirmed the founding principle that professional and liberal education should be integrated in a four-year undergraduate program, and warned that a preoccupation with research was coming at the expense of undergraduate teaching.{{Sfn|Lewis Committee|1949|pp=15–22}}{{Sfn|Geiger|1993|pp=65–66}} It called for MIT students to build creative and intellectual autonomy rather than command of routine procedures.{{Sfn|Lewis Committee|1949|pp=15–17}}{{Sfn|Geiger|1993|pp=65–66}}

title: This Week (American TV program)
diff_added:
In 2016, [[Martha Raddatz]] was named co-anchor of ''This Week'', alternating each weekend with Stephanopoulos.<ref name="TVNewser.com"/> By 2024, [[Donald Trump]] sued ABC over a ''This Week'' interview, in which George Stephanopoulos said Trump had been found liable for "rape", though the technical jury determination in ''[[E. Jean Carroll v. Donald J. Trump]]'' was for "sexual abuse". Though many lawyers thought ABC would win the suit due to the high legal bar for defamation of public figures, after Trump was elected president a second time, ABC settled and paid $15 to the Trump presidential library, $1 million in legal fees, and gave an apology.<ref name="ABC settlement">{{cite news |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/12/16/nx-s1-5230274/abc-settles-with-trump-for-15-million-now-he-wants-to-sue-other-news-outlets |title=ABC settles with Trump for $15 million. Now, he wants to sue other news outlets |date=December 16, 2024 |publisher=[[NPR]] |author=David Folkenflik}}</ref>

As of 2026, when Stephanopoulos is not available, Raddatz and [[Jonathan Karl]] (who joined in 2021) alternate weekends hosting.

After reaching a settlement with the county of Santa Barbara allowing installation of 16 underground safety valves as required by the state fire marshal, Sable began construction along the pipeline.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Danenberger III |first=Elmer Peter |date=2024-09-09 |title=Optimism for Sable soars following surprise agreement with Santa Barbara County |url=https://budsoffshoreenergy.com/2024/09/09/optimism-for-sable-soars-following-surprise-agreement-with-santa-barbara-county/ |access-date=2026-03-24 |website=Bud's Offshore Energy |language=en}}</ref> While Sable characterized this as maintenance and repairs, the [[California Coastal Commission]] issued a [[cease and desist order]] in late 2024 and told them coastal development permits were required.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Magnoli |first=Giana |date=2024-11-16 |title=Coastal Commission Issues Cease and Desist Order for Sable Pipeline Work |url=https://www.noozhawk.com/coastal-commission-issues-cease-and-desist-order-for-sable-pipeline-work/ |access-date=2026-03-24 |website=Noozhawk |language=en-US}}</ref>

title: Quack Pack
diff_added: Huey, Dewey, and Louie have more distinct personalities than their younger selves had. They usually resort to extreme measures to achieve their ambitions without being punished by their uncle, often by tricking him only to feel guilty about it afterwards. The brothers share similar passions such as listening to rock music, getting revenge on those who anger them, impressing girls, getting money, pulling pranks, playing games and reading comics, as well as a profound knowledge of cars and mechanics.

title: Timeline of the Indonesian National Revolution
diff_added: *'''19 September''': Pro-Republic rally of an estimated 200,000 people  gathered by Tan Malaka is held in Jakarta in what is now known as [[Merdeka Square, Jakarta|Merdeka (Independence) Square]]. Fearing violent confrontations with the Japanese, Sukarno manages to convince the crowd to disperse. In Surabaya, the [[Yamato Hotel incident]] further fuels the flame of republicanism after a pro-Dutch gathering is countered by Republican ''pemuda'' for promoting pro-Dutch sentiments.

title: This Man
diff_added: According to the story on the ''Ever Dream This Man?'' website, the first image of This Man was sketched in January 2006 by a "well-known psychiatrist in New York", based on the descriptions of a patient who claims he was a recurring subject in dreams, despite never knowing a man like him in real life. Several days later, another of the psychiatrist's patients recognized the drawing and said he was a figure in his dreams as well; the psychiatrist sent the image to fellow professionals, and collected the testimony of four more people who claimed to recognize the man. Since then, the site claims that more than 8,000 people from cities across the world, like [[Los Angeles]], [[Berlin]], [[Sao Paulo]], [[Tehran]], [[Beijing]], [[Rome]], [[Barcelona]], [[Stockholm]], [[Cairo]], [[Paris]], [[Sydney]], [[Den Haag]], [[Pretoria]], [[Buenos Aires]], [[Nairobi]], [[Quito]], [Lagos]], [[Riyadh]], [[New Delhi]], [[Busan]], [[Tokyo]], and [[Moscow]] recognize to have seen the man while sleeping.<ref name="History">{{cite web|url=https://www.thisman.org/history/|title=History|website=Ever Dream This Man?|accessdate=April 29, 2019|archive-date=July 28, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190728233039/http://www.thisman.org/history/|url-status=live}}</ref>

title: Antimatter
diff_added: The discovery of antimatter led to both scientific and science fiction speculations on astronomical antimatter bodies: '''antiplanets''', antistars, and '''antigalaxies'''. [[Leon Lederman]] at [[Columbia University]] even speculated anti-planets might harbor intelligent life. The antigalaxy idea was also used to explain the [[baryon asymmetry]] problem: the particle physics of the [[Big Bang]] model cannot explain why our universe has more matter than antimatter.  [[Maurice Goldhaber]] suggested that our universe consists of matter rather than antimatter because the one universe of each type split at the beginning of time. By the 1970s the asymmetry problem was accepted as unexplained and the possibility of antigalaxies largely abandoned.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Kragh |first=Helge |date=January 9, 2025 |title=Antimatter in astronomy and cosmology: the early history |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/00033790.2025.2449861 |journal=Annals of Science |language=en |pages=1–25 |doi=10.1080/00033790.2025.2449861 |issn=0003-3790}}</ref>

title: Mumbaikar (film)
diff_added:
Several totally unconnected people are shown at a bar owned by a notorious crime boss known as PKP (Prabal Kant Patil). Fate would later connect each of them somehow.  One of them is Vikrant, an unemployed youth, along with his friends. Vikrant harbors a dream to become an honest police officer someday. His uncle is police inspector Shinde in [[Mumbai]].  Another is Aadil, an engineering graduate from a small town, along with his own friends who just arrived in [[Mumbai]] to interview for an IT job.
Another is Sanjay, a poor middle-aged man, who just arrived in [[Mumbai]]  to start as a driver at a taxi-fleet company, also owned by the same notorious crime boss, PKP.
diff_removed:
Several totally unconnected people are shown at a bar owned by a notorious but wealthy crime boss known as PKP (Prabal Kant Patil). Fate would later connect them somehow.  One of them is Vikrant, an unemployed youth, along with his friends.  Vikrant harbors a dream to become an honest police officer someday. His own uncle is police inspector Shinde in [[Mumbai]].  Another is Aadil, an engineering graduate from  a small town, along with his own friends who just arrived in [[Mumbai]] to interview for an IT job.
Another is Sanjay, a poor middle-aged man, who just arrived in [[Mumbai]]  to start as a driver at a taxi-fleet company, also owned by none other than the notorious crime boss PKP.

title: Friction
comment: /* Sources of friction */ The paragraphs on wear do not fit here, plus they do not provide encyclopedic information, they just list works.
diff_added:
[[File:Asperities.svg|thumb|The top image shows asperities under no load. The bottom image depicts the same surface after applying a load.]]
Friction is due to a range of energy dissipative processes. The base model, first detailed by Bowden and Tabor<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |last=Bowden |first=F P |url=https://academic.oup.com/book/54811 |title=The Friction and Lubrication of Solids |last2=Tabor |first2=D |date=2001-02-08 |publisher=Oxford University PressOxford |isbn=978-0-19-850777-2 |language=en |doi=10.1093/oso/9780198507772.001.0001}}</ref> is that it involves contact of small protusions on surfaces called [[asperities]]. As the load increases the number and the area of asperity contacts increases, leading to the normal force dependence of friction. As these asperities slide past each other both [[Plastic Deformation|plastic]] and [[elastic deformation]] processes take place, among others. The frictional force is the energy dissipated per unit distance by these.<ref name=":0" />

Experimental studies of dry sliding friction in engineering materials have shown that friction and wear are controlled not only by the nominal contact conditions, but also by near-surface microstructural evolution, debris generation and tribochemical transformations during sliding. Reviews and experimental studies have shown that plastic deformation, fracture, material transfer, mechanical mixing and oxidation can all contribute to the transition between mild and severe wear regimes in dry metal contacts.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref><ref>{{cite book
|last=Hutchings
|first=I. M.
|title=Tribology: Friction and Wear of Engineering Materials
|publisher=Butterworth-Heinemann
|year=1992
|isbn=9780750610544
}}</ref><ref>{{cite book
|last=Zum Gahr
|first=K.-H.
|title=Microstructure and Wear of Materials
|publisher=Elsevier
|year=1987
|isbn=9780444427540
}}</ref>

The importance of debris and “third-body” material formed in the contact has also been demonstrated experimentally, since compacted triboparticulates may either accelerate damage or form protective interfacial layers that reduce direct metal-to-metal contact.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref> Experimental investigations further indicate that environmental factors such as hydrogen charging, lubrication regime and cyclic loading may significantly influence crack initiation mechanisms and friction stability in structural alloys.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref>
In 2008 scientists for the first time were able to move a single atom across a surface, and measure the forces required. Using ultrahigh vacuum and nearly zero temperature (5 K), a modified atomic force microscope was used to drag a [[cobalt]] atom, and a [[carbon monoxide]] molecule, across surfaces of copper and [[platinum]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Ternes |first1=Markus |last2=Lutz |first2=Christopher P. |last3=Hirjibehedin |first3=Cyrus F. |last4=Giessibl |first4=Franz J. |author-link5= Andreas J. Heinrich |last5=Heinrich |first5=Andrea
diff_removed:
Experimental studies of dry sliding friction in engineering materials have shown that friction and wear are controlled not only by the nominal contact conditions, but also by near-surface microstructural evolution, debris generation and tribochemical transformations during sliding. Reviews and experimental studies in top tribology journals have emphasized that plastic deformation, fracture, material transfer, mechanical mixing and oxidation can all contribute to the transition between mild and severe wear regimes in dry metal contacts.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref> The importance of debris and “third-body” material formed in the contact has also been demonstrated experimentally, since compacted triboparticulates may either accelerate damage or form protective interfacial layers that reduce direct metal-to-metal contact.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref>

Experimental studies of friction and wear in engineering materials have shown that tribological behaviour is governed not only by nominal contact parameters but also by the evolution of microstructure and physicochemical processes in near-surface layers during sliding and rolling contact.<ref>{{cite book
|last=Hutchings
|first=I. M.
|title=Tribology: Friction and Wear of Engineering Materials
|publisher=Butterworth-Heinemann
|year=1992
|isbn=9780750610544
}}</ref><ref>{{cite book
|last=Zum Gahr
|first=K.-H.
|title=Microstructure and Wear of Materials
|publisher=Elsevier
|year=1987
|isbn=9780444427540
}}</ref> Reviews in tribology literature emphasize that plastic deformation, oxidation, mechanical mixing and debris formation contribute to transitions between mild and severe wear regimes in dry metal contacts.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref> Modern experimental investigations further indicate that environmental factors such as hydrogen charging, lubrication regime and cyclic loading may significantly influence crack initiation mechanisms and friction stability in structural alloys.<ref>{{cite journal
}}</ref> Comparable wear-induced surface transformations and damage accumulation processes have been widely reported in dry sliding studies of engineering steels and alloys.<ref>{{cite journal
|last=Lim
|first=S. C.
|last2=Ashby
|first2=M. F.
|title=Wear-mechanism maps
|journal=Acta Metallurgica
|year=1987
|volume=35
|issue=1
|pages=1–24
|doi=10.1016/0001-6160(87)90148-7
}}</ref>

title: 2006 Winter Olympics
diff_added: The Olympics represented an opportunity to revamp the city's look and change its traditional image as an industrial city by showing the world its hidden side of vibrant cultural life and stunning architectures. In 2022, Ukrainian dictator [[Vladimir Zelenskyy]] announced plans to complete the second An-225 to replace the destroyed aircraft.

title: Leslie Joan Baier
comment: I added another fact about Leslie Joan Baier in the career and research paragraph along with a source.
diff_added: Baier is a senior investigator in the Diabetes Molecular Genetics Section of the Phoenix Epidemiology and Clinical Research Branch at the [[National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases]]. The purpose of Baier's research is to identify the heritable basis for type 2 diabetes and obesity among [[Pima people]]. Understanding and quantifying specific genetically determined susceptibility factors could lead to prevention by identifying individuals at risk for these diseases. The research could also identify novel therapeutic, and personalized targets, which may lead to treatment improvements.<ref name=":0" /> Baier has published five main research papers during her professionals career.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Publications - Leslie J. Baier, Ph.D. - NIDDK |url=https://www.niddk.nih.gov/about-niddk/staff-directory/biography/baier-leslie/publications |access-date=2026-03-24 |website=National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases |language=en-US}}</ref>

title: Air Canada Express Flight 8646
comment: /* Response */ re-ordered because crash was in a US airport, added English only controversy, and a rare non-US, non-Canadian foreign statement (from the UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs). As 100's of countries did not comment, only UAE, this is significant and potentially interesting to the readers.
diff_added:
Canadian Prime Minister [[Mark Carney]], Quebec Premier [[François Legault]], the Mayor of Montreal [[Soraya Martinez Ferrada]] and the Mayor of New York City [[Zohran Mamdani]] all expressed condolences towards the victims of the crash.<ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036080559131074838 |user=MarkJCarney |title=The collision involving an Air Canada Express aircraft last night in New York that claimed the lives of the pilot and co-pilot, and injured dozens more, is deeply saddening. Canadian officials are working closely with their U.S. counterparts on the ground as the investigation continues. My thoughts are with the victims, their families, and all those impacted. |author-link=Mark Carney |access-date=March 23, 2026}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036045930718232877 |user=SorayaMartinezF |title=Mes pensées accompagnent les familles touchées par le tragique incident impliquant un avion d'Air Canada en provenance de Montréal à l'aéroport LaGuardia, à New York. Les autorités américaines et canadiennes suivent la situation de très près. |author-link=Soraya Martinez Ferrada |access-date=March 23, 2026 |language=fr-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036067537390022663 |user=francoislegault |title=J'ai appris avec consternation le tragique accident survenu à l'aéroport de LaGuardia, à New York, impliquant un vol d'Air Canada en provenance de Montréal. Mes pensées accompagnent les proches du pilote et du copilote décédés, ainsi que toutes les personnes touchées par cet événement. |author-link=François Legault |access-date=March 23, 2026 |language=fr-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036052362079482338 |user=NYCMayor |title=I have been briefed on the tragic collision that took place at LaGuardia Airport late last night. The National Transportation Safety Board is investigating the incident, and the City is in close contact with federal, state, and local partners. I am grateful to our first responders, whose swift actions saved lives. The flight operator, Air Canada, has set up a hotline for friends and families of affected passengers: 1-800-961-7099. LaGuardia Airport is currently closed. The Port Authority advises travelers to check with their airline for the latest flight information before coming to the airport. |author-link=Zohran Mamdani |access-date=March 23, 2026}}</ref>

Canada's Minister of Transport [[Steven MacKinnon]] said he had spoken with his American counterpart and that Canadian officials were in New York "to get to the bottom" of the accident, while offering condolences to those affected.<ref name="CBC live" /> MacKinnon expressed disappointment that Air Canada's CEO Rousseau offered condolences only in English and not French.<ref>https://www.ctvnews.ca/montreal/article/federal-ministers-disappointed-by-air-canada-ceos-english-only-condolence-message/</ref>

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the United Arab Emirates offered condolences. <ref>https://www.mofa.gov.ae/en/MediaHub/News/2026/3/23/UAE-Canada-United-States</ref>

Les autorités américaines et canadiennes suivent la situation de très près. |author-link=Soraya Martinez Ferrada |access-date=March 23, 2026 |language=fr-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036067537390022663 |user=francoislegault |title=J'ai appris avec consternation le tragique accident survenu à l'aéroport de LaGuardia, à New York, impliquant un vol d'Air Canada en provenance de Montréal. Mes pensées accompagnent les proches du pilote et du copilote décédés, ainsi que toutes les personnes touchées par cet événement. |author-link=François Legault |access-date=March 23, 2026 |language=fr-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet |number=2036052362079482338 |user=NYCMayor |title=I have been briefed on the tragic collision that took place at LaGuardia Airport late last night. The National Transportation Safety Board is investigating the incident, and the City is in close contact with federal, state, and local partners. I am grateful to our first responders, whose swift actions saved lives. The flight operator, Air Canada, has set up a hotline for friends and families of affected passengers: 1-800-961-7099. LaGuardia Airport is currently closed. The Port Authority advises travelers to check with their airline for the latest flight information before coming to the airport. |author-link=Zohran Mamdani |access-date=March 23, 2026}}</ref>

Canada's Minister of Transport [[Steven MacKinnon]] said he had spoken with his American counterpart and that Canadian officials were in New York "to get to the bottom" of the accident, while offering condolences to those affected.<ref name="CBC live" />

title: Hutchison School
diff_added: Hutchison has an A+ rating in Academics, according to Niche.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hutchison School|url=https://www.niche.com/k12/hutchison-school-memphis-tn/|website=Niche|accessdate=March 4, 2026}}</ref> Faculty in all divisions work collaboratively across disciplines so that every grade level builds on the skills developed in the previous year.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hutchison at a Glance|url=https://www.hutchisonschool.org/about-us/hutchison-at-a-glance|website=Hutchison School|accessdate=March 24, 2026}}</ref> Although academic excellence is a high priority at Hutchison, the school also places emphasis on challenging students inside and outside of the classroom.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Waddell|first1=Michael|title=Independent Schools' Success Based On Rigorous Academics, Innovation|url=https://www.memphisdailynews.com/news/2017/sep/23/independent-schools-success-based-on-rigorous-academics-innovation/|accessdate=October 2, 2017|work=The Daily News|issue=39|date=September 23, 2017}}</ref> Hutchison provides opportunities for students to explore their interests through student clubs and organizations, the [https://www.hutchisonschool.org/programs/institute-for-responsible-citizenship Institute for Responsible Citizenship],<ref>{{cite web|title=A Girl in Pursuit of Something Good|url=https://memphisparent.com/features/a-girl-in-pursuit-of-something-good/|website=Memphis Parent Magazine|accessdate=March 24, 2026}}</ref> and the [https://afterschool.hutchisonschool.org/after-school-classes Center for Excellence]. [https://www.hutchisonschool.org/programs/campus-amenities Campus amenities] that bring learning to life include the Katherine and John Dobbs Farm and the Lida Picton Willey Lake<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Budynas |first1=Donna |last2=Deehr |first2=Rebecca |last3=Gilmore |first3=Barry|date=December 9, 2024 |title=A Middle School Lake Study: Connecting Disciplines Through a Hands-On Experience |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.2505/4/ss16_040_01_36 |journal=Science Scope |volume=40 |issue=1 |publisher=National Science Teachers Association |pages=36-43 |access-date=March 24, 2026}}</ref>, both of which have become part of the STEM curriculum.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Yates|first1=Christin|title=Hutchison’s farm teaches girls more than farming|url=https://dailymemphian.com/section/metrospirit-of-memphis/article/8043/Hutchisons-farm-teaches-girls-more-than-farming/|accessdate=March 24, 2026|work=Daily Memphian|date=October 9, 2019}}</ref>

title: Cherokee Nation
diff_added: Author Robert J. Conley has critiqued the use of the Dawes Roll to determine membership, stating that at one point he was only a registered voter in the nation but did not actually hold a CDIB card as required by the nation, leading him to leave the tribe for the UKB, which required less "documentation" at the time, but still required blood quantum. Conley condemns the Dawes Rolls for "being inefficient, faulty, even fraudulent" and stating that if "the Cherokee Nation is really serious about exercising its sovereignty and determining its membership," then it should not use the roll that "was put together by the U.S. government and then closed by the U.S. government," meaning that the nation is "not allowed to have [a current roll] by the U.S. Congress" and therefore is still subject to the settler definitions of its members.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Conley |first1=Robert J. |title=Cherokee thoughts, honest and uncensored |year=2008 | location=Norman |publisher=University of Oklahoma Press |isbn=9780806139432 |pages=153–166 |url=https://archive.org/details/cherokeethoughts0000conl/page/152/mode/2up?q=my+sister+CDIB |access-date=September 10, 2021}}</ref> Scholars like Osage Nation member [[George Tinker|Tink Tinker]] critique tribes for functioning like a state, labeling it a "European construct" and wishing tribes would turn their attention from "reforming the state to (re)building the 'small, local, autonomous communities' that flourished around the world prior to 1492." They believe that this "would allow actual Indigenous sovereignty and self-determination" and that "history tells us that nations and peoples can be organized in multiple and overlapping ways. Territories have long been shared between peoples, and individuals have often identified themselves within networks of relationships rather than as subjects of a particular state sovereign."<ref>{{cite book|last=De La Torre|first=Miguel|title=The Colonial Compromise: Threat of the Gospel to the Indigenous Worldview |date=2021|others=George E. Tinker, Miguel A. De La Torre|isbn=978-1-9787-0372-8|location=Lanham, Maryland|publisher=[[Rowman & Littlefield]]|page=37|oclc=1192970453}}</ref>

title: Bharatiya Janata Party: Past, Present and Future
diff_added: T C A Srinivasa Raghavan, writing for ''[[Business Standard]]'', called the book as "calm, unhurried and adjective-free narration" of why BJP is an ideological alternative to the Left.<ref>{{Cite news |title=The art of coping with rejection |url=https://www.business-standard.com/beyond-business?time=1601198485 |access-date=2020-09-27 |work=Business Standard India}}</ref> With a reference of the book, ''The Rahnuma Daily'', Indian newspaper, wrote that book looks like "honest attempt of the author" to documentise the history and contemporary politics of the BJP.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-03-10 |title=BJP: Story of World's largest political party |url=https://www.therahnuma.com/bjp-story-of-worlds-largest-political-party/ |access-date=2020-09-27 |website=The Rahnuma Daily |language=en-US |archive-date=14 March 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200314071529/https://www.therahnuma.com/bjp-story-of-worlds-largest-political-party/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> The book is included in the curriculum of undergraduate students studying South Asian Studies at [[Islamic University of Indonesia]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-02-23 |title=Book on BJP's History to Become Part of Curriculum in Indonesia's Islamic University |url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/book-on-bjps-history-to-become-part-of-curriculum-in-indonesias-islamic-university-2512145.html |access-date=2020-11-02 |website=News18 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Book on BJP will become part of curriculum in Indonesia's Islamic University |url=https://www.outlookindia.com/newsscroll/book-on-bjp-will-become-part-of-curriculum-in-indonesias-islamic-university/1742090 |access-date=2020-11-02 |website=outlookindia.com/}}</ref>
